Source_Species.reason: Materials and Methods states the truncated HMG-CoA reductase gene (tHMGR) was from oat.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper identifies oat as Avena strigosa and states tHMGR is from oat.

Applied_Species.reason: tHMGR was coinfiltrated in transient expression assays in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The transient assay host is explicitly Nicotiana benthamiana.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly calls it a truncated 3-hydroxy-3-methyl glutaryl-coenzyme A reductase gene.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: tHMGR was used by coinfiltration/coexpression in transient N. benthamiana expression assays.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper uses tHMGR as a supporting gene to enhance heterologous production and does not test it as the causal determinant of oat wax hopane composition or product specificity.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that tHMGR was included as metabolic support and was not a primary Results focus.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: tHMGR was coexpressed in assays producing hopenol B and hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol, but the paper does not assign these as direct terminal metabolites of tHMGR itself.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The products in the assay are triterpenoids, but tHMGR is not directly shown to make a terminal triterpenoid product.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper says constructs were transferred into pEAQ-HT-DEST1, but does not explicitly describe this as a promoter for tHMGR.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Supported as a helper that supports heterologous triterpene production context rather than a direct phenotype gene.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Hopenol B and hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol are surface wax components, but this function is linked to AsHS genes/products, not directly to tHMGR.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Panicles and sheathes are accumulation sites of the hopane products in oat, but not specifically a field attributable to tHMGR.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves is explicitly described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: tHMGR was coexpressed with AsHS1, AsHS2, and AcHS1, but physical or functional protein interaction is not demonstrated.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data Availability gives accession range OM401318–OM401331 for 13 AsOSC genes and AcHS1; AsHS1 corresponds to AsOSC7, so OM401324 is consistent with the ordered 14-accession series, though not individually spelled out. Directly inferable.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Avena strigosa.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Materials and Methods refers to the A. strigosa S75 genome and cDNA source.

Applied_Species.reason: AsHS1 was analyzed in oat tissues and expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly uses Avena strigosa and Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: S75 is stated for A. strigosa; no variety is given for N. benthamiana.

Enzyme_Name.reason: AsOSC7 was renamed AsHS1 and identified as a hopenol B synthase; it is an oxidosqualene cyclase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number 5.4.99.- appears in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AsHS1 is one of the OSCs and thus an oxidosqualene cyclase/triterpene synthase.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper places AsHS1 in triterpenoid biosynthesis, specifically hopane-type triterpene formation from oxidosqualene.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The results connect AsHS1 to hopane-type pentacyclic triterpenes and wax components.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: AsHS1 catalyzes the cyclization of 2,3-oxidosqualene to the hopane scaffold/product, a branch-determining cyclization step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper states triterpene precursor is (3S)-2,3-oxidosqualene and AsHS1 is an OSC acting on it.

Primary_Product.reason: AsHS1 product was identified as hopenol B by GC-MS and NMR.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly states oxidosqualene passes through dammarane and hopyl C22 cation and is directly deprotonated at C29 to form hopenol B.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: AsHS1 directly forms the target product hopenol B.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: AsHS1 is clearly a key branch/product-determining cyclase, but the paper does not explicitly call it rate-limiting.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: qRT-PCR showed AsHS1 is expressed in young and mature panicles, highest in mature panicles.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper supports panicle-enriched expression in oat and heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AsHS1 directly produces hopenol B, also detected in oat wax.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Hopenol B is described as a pentacyclic triterpene/triterpene alcohol.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Hopenol B is shown as a surface wax component; contribution to drought/cold protection is proposed in Discussion, not directly tested.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Trace amounts of hopenol B were detected in mature panicle surface wax.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support that AsHS1 is linked to hopenol B accumulation in panicle wax.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present in the study: transient expression, GC-MS, NMR, qRT-PCR, wax extraction/GC-MS, mutagenesis, and QM/MM.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported in Results: AsHS1 identified as hopenol B synthase, mature-panicle expression, residue substitutions alter specificity, and C366 acts as general base supported by modeling/mutation.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AsHS1 was coexpressed with tHMGR, but protein interaction was not shown.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome sequencing/RNA-seq and A. strigosa S75 genome v8.0 are explicitly referenced; accession is not provided in this paper text.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both 2-mo-old oat tissue wax analysis and transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana are described.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The construct vector pEAQ-HT/pEAQ-HT-DEST1 is stated, but a specific promoter is not named.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering utility is a reasonable implication, but this application value is not directly demonstrated in Results.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: No tradeoffs or cuticle property effects were experimentally reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: AsHS1 corresponds to AsOSC7; Y410, F257, F729, and C366 roles, plus C366D/C366G behavior, are all described in Results and figures/SI.

corrections.reason: No EC number 5.4.99.- appears in the paper.
